description: 6-agent expert review: panel selection, 3 structural + 3 expert personas, synthesis

steps.description:
Read {{project_root}}/reviewers.yaml and select 3 reviewers for the plan at {{plan}}.

RULES:
1. Select EXACTLY 3 reviewers from DIFFERENT categories
2. MUST include exactly 1 from adversarial_error_finders
3. If reviewers.yaml has panel templates, check if one matches the plan
4. If previous_panel is non-empty, ROTATE: replace at least 1 reviewer
5. Prefer confidence A > B > C
6. For each reviewer, generate a domain-specific hard question based on the plan content

PREVIOUS PANEL: {{previous_panel}}

Write result to this issue's notes as JSON:
{"panel": [{"name": "...", "category": "...", "role": "...", "domain": "...", "question": "..."}], "reason": "...", "panel_template_used": "..."}

STOPPING CONDITIONS: After writing panel JSON to notes, stop.
steps.title: Select review panel for {{plan}}

steps.description:
ROLE: ADVOCATE. Assume the plan is correct. Find its strengths and single point of failure.

Plan: {{plan}}
Project root: {{project_root}}

Read the plan file first. Read {{project_root}}/CLAUDE.md for project context and {{project_root}}/docs/reference/proven-facts.md for established results.

You have full tool access. Read code, run tests, verify claims.

Write your assessment to this issue's notes.

STOPPING CONDITIONS: After identifying key strength and key vulnerability with evidence, write notes and stop.
steps.title: Advocate: strengths and vulnerabilities of {{plan}}

steps.description:
ROLE: CHALLENGER. Assume the plan has a flaw. Find it.

Plan: {{plan}}
Project root: {{project_root}}

Read the plan file first. Read {{project_root}}/CLAUDE.md for project context and {{project_root}}/docs/reference/proven-facts.md for established results.

Find: weakest assumption, implicit step most likely wrong, specific degenerate case or edge case.

You have full tool access. Read code, construct counterexamples.

Write your assessment to this issue's notes.

STOPPING CONDITIONS: After finding at least one potential flaw (or proving none exist) with evidence, write notes and stop.
steps.title: Challenger: find flaws in {{plan}}
Computational adversary: verify claims in {{plan}}

steps.description:
Read the select-panel step's notes to find YOUR assigned persona and question (panel[0]).

Adopt that persona. Review the plan at {{plan}} from that domain expertise.
Read {{project_root}}/CLAUDE.md for context and {{project_root}}/docs/reference/proven-facts.md. Do NOT question proven facts.

Focus ONLY on your assigned question. Be specific: cite line numbers, identify exact steps that concern you, propose specific tests.

Do NOT use Edit or Write on project files. Read-only.

Write your review to this issue's notes.

STOPPING CONDITIONS: After completing your domain-specific review, write notes and stop.

Read {{project_root}}/CLAUDE.md for context and {{project_root}}/docs/reference/proven-facts.md. Do NOT question proven facts.

steps.title: Expert review 3 of {{plan}}

steps.description:
ROLE: LEAD SYNTHESIZER. Read notes from ALL 6 preceding steps.

Epic: {{epic}}

## Issue Classification (CRITICAL — read before weighting)

Classify EVERY finding into exactly one category:

| Category | Definition | Examples | Blocks APPROVED? |
|----------|-----------|----------|------------------|
| DESIGN-BLOCKING | Architecture is wrong, invariant violated, approach fundamentally flawed | Missing error path causes data loss, algorithm has wrong complexity class, security vulnerability | YES |
| IMPLEMENTATION-NOTE | Valid concern but addressable during coding without changing the design | Edge case needs a guard clause, error message could be better, missing retry logic | NO — include as note |
| STYLE | Naming, formatting, documentation wording | Variable name unclear, comment could be expanded | NO — ignore |

A finding is DESIGN-BLOCKING only if implementing the plan AS WRITTEN would produce incorrect, unsafe, or fundamentally broken results. If the plan's approach is sound but a reviewer identifies an edge case that a competent implementer would naturally handle, that is an IMPLEMENTATION-NOTE.

## Weighting
- Computational REFUTED a claim: CRITICAL -> REJECTED unless explainable
- Challenger found specific flaw with evidence: HIGH -> DESIGN-BLOCKING only if it invalidates the approach
- 2+ experts agree on a concern: HIGH -> classify before blocking
- 1 expert concern, others disagree: MEDIUM -> IMPLEMENTATION-NOTE unless proven design flaw
- Advocate found vulnerability: MEDIUM (note as risk)

## Conflict resolution
- If advocate and challenger disagree: trust computational evidence. No evidence -> flag for user.
- If expert contradicts proven-facts.md: trust proven-facts, note disagreement.
- NEVER resolve by majority vote. Resolve by evidence.

## Blocking threshold
REJECT only if there is at least 1 DESIGN-BLOCKING issue with concrete evidence (not hypothetical). Concerns like 'what if X fails' are IMPLEMENTATION-NOTEs unless the plan explicitly precludes handling X.

## Plan-editing boundary
You may fix ONLY anti-pattern language (literal string replacement). You may NOT modify mathematical content, add/remove steps, or change the approach. If math needs fixing -> REJECTED.

## Verdict
Write verdict to this issue's notes as:
VERDICT: APPROVED|REJECTED|INCOMPLETE

For APPROVED, also list IMPLEMENTATION-NOTEs the implementer should address.
For REJECTED, list ONLY DESIGN-BLOCKING issues with evidence. Do not pad with IMPLEMENTATION-NOTEs.

Then structured summary with panel names, each agent's key finding, and evidence.

After writing verdict:
- If APPROVED: run `bd comments add {{epic}} "APPROVED: <one-line summary>"` then find the human gate blocking implementation and run `bd gate resolve <gate-id>`.
- If REJECTED: run `bd comments add {{epic}} "REJECTED: <specific fixes required>"`. Do NOT resolve any gate.
- If INCOMPLETE: run `bd comments add {{epic}} "INCOMPLETE: <what's missing>"`. Do NOT resolve any gate.

STOPPING CONDITIONS: After writing verdict and running the appropriate bd commands, stop.
steps.title: Synthesize review verdict for {{plan}}

epic.description: Parent epic bead ID. Verdict is commented here; gate resolved on APPROVED.

plan.description: Path to plan file or bead ID being reviewed

previous_panel.description: JSON of previous review panel (for rotation). Empty string if first review.
